---
title: "STAT293_Kurum_FinalProject"
author: "Yijia Xue"
date: "2026-02-22"
output: pdf_document
---
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(ggplot2)
library(survival)
library(splines)
library(nlme)
library(GLMMadaptive)
library(JMbayes2)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(survminer)

load("ckd.rdata")
prot<-ckd_data$prot
haem<-ckd_data$haem
gfr<-ckd_data$gfr
surv<-ckd_data$surv
```

# Data Preparation

```{r}
# Verify event indicator coding
unique(surv$failure)
unique(prot$proteinuria)
summary(prot$years)
summary(gfr$years)
summary(haem$years)

count_followups <- function(data) {
  data %>%
    group_by(id) %>%
    summarise(n_followups = n(), .groups = "drop")
}

summary(count_followups(gfr)$n_followups)
summary(count_followups(haem)$n_followups)
summary(count_followups(prot)$n_followups)

gfr_sub  <- gfr %>% select(id, years, gfr)
haem_sub <- haem %>% select(id, years, haematocrit)

merged_df <- inner_join(gfr_sub, haem_sub, by = c("id", "years"))
cor(merged_df$gfr,
    merged_df$haematocrit,
    use = "complete.obs")


subject_means <- merged_df %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_gfr = mean(gfr, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean_haem = mean(haematocrit, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cor(subject_means$mean_gfr,
    subject_means$mean_haem,
    use = "complete.obs")
```

# EDA

```{r}
# =========================
# EDA
# =========================

# -------------------------
# 1) Baseline summaries + survival overview
# -------------------------
summary(surv$age)
summary(surv$weight)
if ("gender" %in% names(surv)) table(surv$gender)
if ("sex" %in% names(surv)) table(surv$sex)

table(surv$failure)
mean(surv$failure)
summary(surv$fuyears)

km_fit <- survfit(Surv(fuyears, failure) ~ 1, data = surv)
plot(km_fit, xlab = "Time (years)", ylab = "Survival probability",
     main = "Kaplan–Meier Curve for Graft Survival")

# -------------------------
# 2) Distributions (continuous biomarkers)
# -------------------------
gfr_clean  <- gfr  %>% filter(is.finite(gfr), !is.na(gfr))
haem_clean <- haem %>% filter(is.finite(haematocrit), !is.na(haematocrit))

ggplot(haem_clean, aes(x = haematocrit)) +
  geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)), bins = 30, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_density(size = 1) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Haematocrit", x = "Haematocrit (%)", y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal()

ggplot(gfr_clean, aes(x = gfr)) +
  geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)), bins = 30, alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_density(size = 1) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of GFR", x = "GFR (ml/min/1.73m2)", y = "Density") +
  theme_minimal()

# -------------------------
# 3) Proteinuria prevalence over time
# -------------------------
prot_clean <- prot %>%
  mutate(proteinuria = as.integer(as.character(proteinuria))) %>%  
  filter(is.finite(years), !is.na(proteinuria))

prot_prev <- prot_clean %>%
  group_by(years) %>%
  summarise(prevalence = mean(proteinuria), n = n(), .groups = "drop")

ggplot(prot_prev, aes(x = years, y = prevalence)) +
  geom_line(size = 1) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(title = "Prevalence of Proteinuria Over Time",
       x = "Time (years)", y = "Prevalence") +
  theme_minimal()

# -------------------------
# 4) Longitudinal trends + heterogeneity
# -------------------------
set.seed(1)
ids_gfr  <- sample(unique(gfr_clean$id),  min(30, length(unique(gfr_clean$id))))
ids_haem <- sample(unique(haem_clean$id), min(30, length(unique(haem_clean$id))))

ggplot(gfr_clean %>% filter(id %in% ids_gfr),
       aes(x = years, y = gfr, group = id)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(aes(group = 1), se = FALSE, size = 1.1) +
  labs(title = "GFR Trajectories Over Time (subset)", x = "Time (years)", y = "GFR") +
  theme_minimal()

ggplot(haem_clean %>% filter(id %in% ids_haem),
       aes(x = years, y = haematocrit, group = id)) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(aes(group = 1), se = FALSE, size = 1.1) +
  labs(title = "Haematocrit Trajectories Over Time (subset)",
       x = "Time (years)", y = "Haematocrit (%)") +
  theme_minimal()


ggplot(gfr_clean, aes(x = years, y = gfr)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.07) +
  stat_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ ns(x, 3), se = FALSE, size = 1.1) +
  labs(title = "GFR Trend (Natural Spline)", x = "Time (years)", y = "GFR") +
  theme_minimal()

# -------------------------
# 5) Exploratory association: trends by failure status
# -------------------------
ggplot(gfr_clean, aes(x = years, y = gfr, color = factor(failure))) +
  stat_smooth(se = FALSE, size = 1.2) +
  labs(title = "GFR Trend by Failure Status",
       x = "Time (years)", y = "GFR", color = "Failure") +
  theme_minimal()

ggplot(haem_clean, aes(x = years, y = haematocrit, color = factor(failure))) +
  stat_smooth(se = FALSE, size = 1.2) +
  labs(title = "Haematocrit Trend by Failure Status",
       x = "Time (years)", y = "Haematocrit (%)", color = "Failure") +
  theme_minimal()

# -------------------------
# 6) Missingness summary
# -------------------------
miss_prop <- tibble(
  biomarker = c("GFR", "Haematocrit", "Proteinuria"),
  prop_missing = c(mean(is.na(gfr$gfr) | !is.finite(gfr$gfr)),
                   mean(is.na(haem$haematocrit) | !is.finite(haem$haematocrit)),
                   mean(is.na(prot$proteinuria)))
)
miss_prop

gfr$R <- ifelse(is.na(gfr), 0, 1)
colMeans(gfr$R) #missingness only in gfr

gfr_df <- gfr%>%
  arrange(id, years) %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  mutate(lag_gfr = lag(gfr))

summary(glm(R[,"gfr"] ~ lag_gfr +years+failure,
    family = binomial,
    data = gfr_df))

prot$R <- ifelse(is.na(prot), 0, 1)
colMeans(prot$R)

prot_df <- prot%>%
  arrange(id, years) %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  mutate(lag_prot = lag(proteinuria))


summary(glm(R[,"proteinuria"] ~ lag_prot + years+failure,
    family = binomial,
    data = prot_df))


haem$R <- ifelse(is.na(haem), 0, 1)
colMeans(haem$R)

haem_df <- haem%>%
  arrange(id, years) %>%
  group_by(id) %>%
  mutate(lag_haem = lag(haematocrit))


summary(glm(R[,"haematocrit"] ~ lag_haem + years+failure,
    family = binomial,
    data = haem_df))
```




# Model Fitting

## longitudinal submodels

```{r}
# Common longitudinal dataset

joint_long <- full_join(
  gfr %>% select(id, years, gfr),
  haem %>% select(id, years, haematocrit),
  by = c("id", "years")
) %>%
  full_join(
    prot %>%
      select(id, years, proteinuria) %>%
      mutate(proteinuria = as.numeric(as.character(proteinuria))),
    by = c("id", "years")
  ) %>%
  left_join(
    surv %>% select(id, failure, fuyears, age, weight, gender) %>% distinct(id, .keep_all = TRUE),
    by = "id"
  ) %>%
  arrange(id, years)

joint_long$gender <- factor(joint_long$gender)

# GFR model (with spline)
lme_gfr <- lme(
  fixed = gfr ~ ns(years, df = 3), # cubic spline enters here
  random = ~ years | id,
  data = joint_long,
  na.action = na.exclude,
  control = lmeControl(opt = "optim")
)

# Haematocrit Model 
lme_haem <- lme(
  fixed = haematocrit ~ years,
  random = ~ years | id,
  data = joint_long,
  na.action = na.exclude,
  control = lmeControl(opt = "optim")
)

# Proteinuria Model (mixed logistic model)
mixed_prot <- mixed_model(
  fixed = proteinuria ~ years,
  random = ~ 1 | id,
  data = joint_long,
  family = binomial(),
  na.action = na.exclude
)
```

##survival submodel

```{r}
surv_dat <- surv %>%
  select(id, fuyears, failure, age, weight, gender) %>%
  distinct(id, .keep_all = TRUE)

surv_dat$gender <- factor(surv_dat$gender)

cox_fit <- coxph(
  Surv(fuyears, failure) ~ age + weight + gender,
  data = surv_dat,
  x = TRUE
)

```

## Joint Model

```{r}
joint_fit <- jm(
  cox_fit,
  list(lme_gfr, lme_haem, mixed_prot),
  time_var = "years"
)
summary(joint_fit)
```
# Model Diagnosis

```{r}

# 1. GFR model diagnosis

# ---- create diagnostic dataframe ----
diag_gfr <- data.frame(
  fitted = fitted(lme_gfr, level = 0),
  residuals = resid(lme_gfr, type = "normalized")
)

diag_gfr <- diag_gfr %>%
  filter(!is.na(fitted), !is.na(residuals))

# ---- 1A. Residuals vs fitted ----
ggplot(diag_gfr, aes(x = fitted, y = residuals)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "GFR: Residuals vs Fitted",
    x = "Fitted values",
    y = "Normalized residuals"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# ---- 1B. Histogram of residuals ----
ggplot(diag_gfr, aes(x = residuals)) +
  geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)), bins = 30,
                 alpha = 0.6, fill = "grey70", color = "black") +
  geom_density(linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "GFR: Histogram of Residuals",
    x = "Normalized residuals",
    y = "Density"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# ---- 1C. QQ plot of residuals ----
ggplot(diag_gfr, aes(sample = residuals)) +
  stat_qq(alpha = 0.3) +
  stat_qq_line(linewidth = 1, color="red") +
  labs(
    title = "GFR: Q-Q Plot of Residuals"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


# ---- 1D. QQ plot of random intercepts ----
re_gfr <- ranef(lme_gfr)
re_gfr_df <- data.frame(
  intercept = re_gfr[, 1],
  slope = re_gfr[, 2]
)

ggplot(re_gfr_df, aes(sample = intercept)) +
  stat_qq(alpha = 0.4) +
  stat_qq_line(linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "GFR: Q-Q Plot of Random Intercepts"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


# 2. Haematocrit model diagnosis
diag_haem <- data.frame(
  fitted = fitted(lme_haem, level = 0),
  residuals = resid(lme_haem, type = "normalized")
)

diag_haem <- diag_haem %>%
  filter(!is.na(fitted), !is.na(residuals))

# ---- 2A. Residuals vs fitted ----
ggplot(diag_haem, aes(x = fitted, y = residuals)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.25) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "red") +
  labs(
    title = "Haem: Residuals vs Fitted",
    x = "Fitted values",
    y = "Normalized residuals"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# ---- 2B. Histogram of residuals ----
ggplot(diag_haem, aes(x = residuals)) +
  geom_histogram(aes(y = after_stat(density)), bins = 30,
                 alpha = 0.6, fill = "grey70", color = "black") +
  geom_density(linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Haem: Histogram of Residuals",
    x = "Normalized residuals",
    y = "Density"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

# ---- 2C. QQ plot of residuals ----
ggplot(diag_haem, aes(sample = residuals)) +
  stat_qq(alpha = 0.3) +
  stat_qq_line(linewidth = 1, color="red") +
  labs(
    title = "Haem: Q-Q Plot of Residuals"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


# ---- 2D. QQ plot of random intercepts ----
re_haem <- ranef(lme_haem)
re_haem_df <- data.frame(
  intercept = re_haem[, 1],
  slope = re_haem[, 2]
)

ggplot(re_haem_df, aes(sample = intercept)) +
  stat_qq(alpha = 0.4) +
  stat_qq_line(linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    title = "Haem: Q-Q Plot of Random Intercepts"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()



# 3. Proteinuria model diagnosis

## Predicted probabilities
prot_diag <- prot_dat
prot_diag$pred_prob <- as.numeric(predict(mixed_prot, type = "mean_subject"))
prot_diag <- prot_diag %>%
  filter(!is.na(pred_prob), !is.na(proteinuria))

# ---- 3A. Predicted probability distribution ----
ggplot(prot_diag, aes(x = pred_prob)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30,
                 fill = "grey70",
                 alpha = 0.7) +
  labs(
    title = "Proteinuria: Predicted Probability Distribution",
    x = "Predicted probability"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


# ---- 3B. Observed vs predicted ----

ggplot(prot_diag, aes(x = pred_prob, y = proteinuria)) +
  geom_jitter(height = 0.05, alpha = 0.2) +
  labs(
    title = "Proteinuria: Observed vs Predicted",
    x = "Predicted probability",
    y = "Observed proteinuria"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()


# 4. Cox model diagnosis
ph_test <- cox.zph(cox_fit)

print(ph_test)



# ---- 4A. Schoenfeld residuals ----
plot(ph_test)

summary(joint_fit)
library(survminer)

# ---- 4B.log(-log) survival curves ---- (checking the survival models's PH assumption is valid)
ggsurvplot(
  survfit(Surv(fuyears, failure) ~ gender, data = surv_dat),
  fun = "cloglog"
)
## The curves for male and female subjects were approximately parallel over time, suggesting that the proportional hazards assumption for gender is reasonable.
```
# Model Estimate (just for reference)

```{r}

# # ----------------------------
# # GFR longitudinal submodel
# # ----------------------------
# # fixed effects
# gfr_fixef <- summary(lme_gfr)$tTable
# gfr_fixef
# 
# # random-effects variance-covariance
# VarCorr(lme_gfr)
# 
# 
# # ----------------------------
# # Haematocrit longitudinal submodel
# # ----------------------------
# # fixed effects
# haem_fixef <- summary(lme_haem)$tTable
# haem_fixef
# 
# # random-effects variance-covariance
# VarCorr(lme_haem)
# 
# # ----------------------------
# # Proteinuria longitudinal submodel
# # ----------------------------
# 
# # fixed effects table
# prot_coef <- summary(mixed_prot)$coef_table
# prot_coef
# 
# # random effects
# summary(mixed_prot)$D
# 
# # ----------------------------
# # Joint model summary
# # ----------------------------
# s <- summary(joint_fit)
# 
# # survival part of the joint model
# s$Survival
# 
# assoc_tab <- s$Survival[grep("^value\\(", rownames(s$Survival)), , drop = FALSE]
# assoc_tab
# 
# # baseline covariates only (exclude value(...) rows)
# surv_tab <- s$Survival[!grepl("^value\\(", rownames(s$Survival)), , drop = FALSE]
# surv_tab

```


# Results

```{r}
# =========================================================
# TABLE 1: Longitudinal submodels
# =========================================================

# ----- GFR fixed effects -----
gfr_fixef <- summary(lme_gfr)$tTable

gfr_table <- data.frame(
  Outcome = "GFR",
  Term = rownames(gfr_fixef),
  Estimate = gfr_fixef[, "Value"],
  SE = gfr_fixef[, "Std.Error"],
  Statistic = gfr_fixef[, "t-value"],
  P_value = gfr_fixef[, "p-value"],
  row.names = NULL
)

# make names cleaner
gfr_table$Term <- c(
  "Intercept",
  "Spline(years) 1",
  "Spline(years) 2",
  "Spline(years) 3"
)

# ----- Haematocrit fixed effects -----
haem_fixef <- summary(lme_haem)$tTable

haem_table <- data.frame(
  Outcome = "Haematocrit",
  Term = rownames(haem_fixef),
  Estimate = haem_fixef[, "Value"],
  SE = haem_fixef[, "Std.Error"],
  Statistic = haem_fixef[, "t-value"],
  P_value = haem_fixef[, "p-value"],
  row.names = NULL
)

# clean names if desired
haem_table$Term[haem_table$Term == "(Intercept)"] <- "Intercept"
haem_table$Term[haem_table$Term == "years"] <- "Years since transplant"

# ----- Proteinuria fixed effects -----
prot_coef <- summary(mixed_prot)$coef_table

prot_table <- data.frame(
  Outcome = "Proteinuria",
  Term = rownames(prot_coef),
  Estimate = prot_coef[, "Estimate"],
  SE = prot_coef[, "Std.Err"],
  Statistic = prot_coef[, "z-value"],
  P_value = prot_coef[, "p-value"],
  row.names = NULL
)
prot_table$Term[prot_table$Term == "(Intercept)"] <- "Intercept"
prot_table$Term[prot_table$Term == "years"] <- "Years since transplant"

# ----- Combine into one table -----
long_table <- bind_rows(gfr_table, haem_table, prot_table)

# nicer p-values
long_table$P_value <- ifelse(long_table$P_value < 0.001, "<0.001",
                             sprintf("%.3f", long_table$P_value))
long_table <- long_table %>%
  mutate(
    Estimate = round(Estimate, 3),
    SE = round(SE, 3),
    Statistic = round(Statistic, 2)
  )

kable(
  long_table,
  caption = "Fixed-effects estimates for the longitudinal submodels.",
  col.names = c("Outcome", "Term", "Estimate", "SE", "Statistic", "P-value"),
  align = c("l", "l", "r", "r", "r", "r"),
  booktabs = TRUE
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("striped", "hold_position"),
    full_width = FALSE,
    position = "center"
  )

# =========================================================
# TABLE 2: Joint Survival Model
# =========================================================

s <- summary(joint_fit)

joint_surv_table <- data.frame(
  Term = rownames(s$Survival),
  Estimate = s$Survival[, "Mean"],
  SE = s$Survival[, "StDev"],
  CI_lower = s$Survival[, "2.5%"],
  CI_upper = s$Survival[, "97.5%"],
  P_value = s$Survival[, "P"],
  Rhat = s$Survival[, "Rhat"],
  row.names = NULL
)

joint_surv_table$Term <- c(
  "Age",
  "Weight",
  "Male",
  "Current GFR",
  "Current Haematocrit",
  "Current Proteinuria"
)

joint_surv_table$P_value <- ifelse(joint_surv_table$P_value < 0.001, "<0.001",
                                   sprintf("%.3f", joint_surv_table$P_value))


kable(
  joint_surv_table,
  caption = "Posterior summaries for the survival component of the joint model.",
  col.names = c("Term", "Estimate", "SE", "2.5%", "97.5%", "P-value", "Rhat"),
  align = c("l", "r", "r", "r", "r", "r", "r"),
  booktabs = TRUE,
  escape = FALSE
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("striped", "hold_position"),
    full_width = FALSE,
    position = "center"
  )


# =========================================================
# TABLE 3: Hazard Ratios (exp of table 2)
# =========================================================

hr_table <- data.frame(
  Term = rownames(s$Survival),
  HR = exp(s$Survival[, "Mean"]),
  CI_lower = exp(s$Survival[, "2.5%"]),
  CI_upper = exp(s$Survival[, "97.5%"]),
  P_value = s$Survival[, "P"],
  row.names = NULL
)

hr_table$Term <- c(
  "Age",
  "Weight",
  "Male",
  "Current GFR",
  "Current Haematocrit",
  "Current Proteinuria"
)

hr_table$P_value <- ifelse(hr_table$P_value < 0.001, "<0.001",
                           sprintf("%.3f", hr_table$P_value))

kable(
  hr_table,
  caption = "Hazard ratios for baseline covariates and association parameters in the joint model.",
  col.names = c("Term", "HR", "2.5%", "97.5%", "P-value"),
  align = c("l", "r", "r", "r", "r"),
  booktabs = TRUE,
  escape = FALSE
) %>%
  kable_styling(
    latex_options = c("striped", "hold_position"),
    full_width = FALSE,
    position = "center"
  )


fit_km <- survfit(Surv(fuyears, failure) ~ gender, data = surv_dat)

# (this plot may or maynot used in our result)
ggsurvplot(
  fit_km,
  risk.table = TRUE,
  conf.int = TRUE,
  xlab = "Years since transplant",
  ylab = "Survival probability",
  legend.title = "Gender"
)

# forest plot of hazard ratio, also is valid for secondary research question
ggplot(hr_table, aes(x = Term, y = HR)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = CI_lower, ymax = CI_upper), width = 0.2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(
    title = "Hazard ratios from the joint model",
    y = "Hazard ratio",
    x = ""
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
